title: sphaeralcea densiflora var. viscida
intro: Sphaeralcea densiflora var. viscida, also known as sticky globe mallow, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It belongs to the mallow family and is known for its bright orange flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Sphaeralcea densiflora var. viscida can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems.
